### Post by rmnsf936 on 2008-02-05

Here's my xorg.conf:
```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#

# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC OOption "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"NLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SDM-S95DR/FR"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"SDM-S95DR/FR"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1280x960"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

```

### Post by perfecttao on 2008-02-05
Depends on the version of Ubuntu you are using, but generally this has to do with the theme you are using (on Compiz).  Quick fix = install Emerald Theme Manager and restart the X Server (CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE)

:)

---

### Post by rmnsf936 on 2008-02-05
I already have emerald theme manager installed. When I try to run emerald from terminal I get this error:
```
emerald: Could not acquire decoration manager selection on screen 0 display ":1.0"

```

---

### Post by vikasvishnu on 2008-02-06
Hmm.. I believe it a bug in the current Emerald theme you are using. Have you tried using a different Emerald theme? You can download them from Gnome-look.org or similar websites and install them using Emerald-Theme-Manager. Try changing the theme and see how it goes. 

Also it could be an issue with your current Compiz profile. Just try renaming the .compiz folder under your Home directory and restart X (press Ctrl + Alt + Backspace). This should create a new profile for you under the .compiz folder and might solve your issue.

---

### Post by Yes_It's_Me on 2008-02-06
There was an update for compiz packages today which i believe caused this issue. I updated my system and had the same issue, all the regular fixes do NOT work at all.

It was working before this update and as the original poster posted not very long ago it seems like maybe the update wasn't tested correctly.

however, i am running hardy instead of gutsy...

---

### Post by perfecttao on 2008-02-06
Another option would be to change the theme you are using in the "normal" gnome theme manager, removing the emerald theme altogether.

Go into Preferences->Appearance and change the Theme - it may disable Desktop effects, in which case just re-enable in Appearance->Visual Effects

---
